headline: Mag 7 Stocks Shed Nearly $3 Trillion This Month
summary: A massive rotation at the expense of big AI spenders has the Magnificent Seven on pace to shed $3 trillion this month.  Collectively, Tesla, Apple, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Amazon.com, and Alphabet have lost $2.99 trillion in market cap so far in June, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.

The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.

headline: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash
summary: Heading into Thursday’s session, Microsoft stock had fallen more than 24% in 2026, putting it on track for its steepest first-half decline since a nearly 32% drop in 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  For much of its history, Microsoft was best known as the pioneer behind the Windows operating system and wildly popular Office software.  Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

raw.headline: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash
raw.summary: Heading into Thursday’s session, Microsoft stock had fallen more than 24% in 2026, putting it on track for its steepest first-half decline since a nearly 32% drop in 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  For much of its history, Microsoft was best known as the pioneer behind the Windows operating system and wildly popular Office software.  Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

headline: Why the Dow Is Soaring But the Nasdaq Is Falling
summary: The stock market is leaving Big Tech stocks in the dust. The Dow surged 733 points, or 1.4%, while the S&P 500 gained 0.3%. The Nasdaq, which was up nearly 1% at the open, was down 0.6%. More than four-in-five S&P 500 stocks were set to close higher, but the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF dropped 2%.

headline: Is SpaceX's $25B debt sale raising bubble concerns on Wall Street?
summary: SpaceX (SPCX) stock continues to struggle to gain back some ground after shedding nearly all of its post-IPO gains. The Elon Musk-led company's $25 billion bond sale is now raising concerns on Wall Street about a possible market bubble, according to the Financial Times. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Epistrophy Capital Research chief market strategist Cory Johnson to weigh in on all of this.

headline: Why Amazon Is Investing Another $13 Billion in India’s AI Data Centers
summary: Amazon  has committed an additional $13 billion to expand its artificial-intelligence and cloud infrastructure in India as it doubles down on the world’s most populous country.  This latest infusion brings  Amazon’s  total planned investment in India to $48 billion between 2026 and 2030.  The fresh capital will be used specifically to expand data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad, Amazon said.

headline: Jumio Is the First Identity Intelligence Provider to Offer Global Digital ID Acceptance at Scale
summary: SUNNYVALE, Calif., June 25, 2026--Jumio, the leading provider of AI-powered identity intelligence, today announced that it has significantly expanded support for digital IDs across the globe, allowing Jumio customers to accept digital IDs in more than 60 countries and territories when onboarding new users, helping customers drive conversions and meet compliance mandates.

headline: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
summary: Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should ‌be designated gatekeepers under EU ‌rules aimed at reining in the power ​of Big Tech, EU antitrust regulators said on Thursday. Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, the two largest ‌cloud providers globally, ⁠should be designated gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act ⁠which sets out a list of dos and don'ts to ensure ​a level ​playing field, ​the European Commission ‌said. The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

raw.headline: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say

The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

headline: AI capex under-investment is a bigger risk than saturation: Dan Newman
summary: Daniel Newman of The Futurum Group compares Micron’s Q3 earnings print to Nvidia’s performance in 2023-2024. Newman says underinvesting in AI can pose a greater risk than overinvesting. He believes AI-driven demand and market rally seem sustainable, with The Futurum Group projecting AI capital expenditure to reach $5.7 trillion in 2035.
